action.reasoning: Observation: training succeeded quickly, but local evaluation failed because planner.py loads checkpoints strictly while older checkpoints use the previous NeighborScorer key layout (net.* vs backbone/head.*). Analysis: this is a compatibility bug, not a planner-quality issue; it blocked eval submission. The new hybrid checkpoint likely has the new format, but robust predict.py must load both old and new checkpoints offline. Decision: patch model/planner loading for backward compatibility, add a local eval command on the new hybrid checkpoint, and if it runs successfully submit eval immediately to recover iteration efficiency.
action.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('planner.py')
text = p.read_text()
text = text.replace('from model import NeighborScorer\n', 'from model import NeighborScorer, remap_legacy_state_dict\n')
old = '            state = torch.load(ckpt_path, map_location=self.device)\n            if isinstance(state, dict) and "model" in state:\n                self.model.load_state_dict(state["model"])\n                self.use_model = True\n'
new = '            state = torch.load(ckpt_path, map_location=self.device)\n            if isinstance(state, dict) and "model" in state:\n                model_state = remap_legacy_state_dict(state["model"])\n                self.model.load_state_dict(model_state, strict=False)\n                self.use_model = True\n'
if old not in text:
    raise SystemExit('target block not found in planner.py')
text = text.replace(old, new)
p.write_text(text)
PY
python local_eval.py --checkpoint checkpoints/hybrid --data_dir /data --max_maps 90 --output hybrid_local_eval.json

command_output.stderr:
usage: local_eval.py [-h] --checkpoint CHECKPOINT [--data_dir DATA_DIR]
                     [--limit_per_type LIMIT_PER_TYPE] --output OUTPUT
local_eval.py: error: unrecognized arguments: --max_maps 90
